phaseGoals.Create/Scaffold: Create the sample workspace and initialize the azd project.
phaseGoals.Local Run: Run the sample locally and verify the protocol path.
phaseGoals.Deploy: Package, push, and deploy the hosted agent.
phaseGoals.Remote Invoke: Call the remote agent and verify the result.
phaseGoals.Update & Redeploy: Make a code change, redeploy, verify new behavior.

All 3 substeps passed. No follow-up work identified.

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

Command:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8088/invocations -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{ "message": "Hello!" }'

Output:

{"echo":"🔊 Echo: Hello!"}

Exit code: 0 Duration: <1s Status: ✅ PASS — curl fallback also succeeded with expected echo response

Note: Used port 8088 (not 8094) since the .NET app did not honor the PORT env var. The curl response matches the azd ai agent invoke --local result.

Server stopped with Ctrl-C after invoke. Server logs confirmed both requests (azd invoke and curl) were processed as HTTP 200.

Substep: local_run.local_invoke_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-15/echo-nonstreaming-dotnet-invocations/src && azd ai agent invoke --local '{"message": "Hello!"}'

P05 Remote Invoke FAIL. azd ai agent invoke returned HTTP 404 ResourceNotFound ("Subdomain does not map to a resource") on the invocations protocol endpoint. The versioned endpoint (/versions/15) exists (returns 401), confirming the agent was deployed, but the protocol routing is broken — cascade from P04 postdeploy RBAC hook failure (AzureDeveloperCLICredential unavailable in JIT context). 404 is not an auth symptom so the RBAC retry rule did not apply. Log written to P05-remote-invoke.log. No follow-up work identified — root cause is the known RBAC hook issue from P04.

Substep: monitor.azd_monitor_attempt — ❌ FAIL (CLI limitation)

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-15/echo-nonstreaming-dotnet-invocations/src && azd ai agent monitor --tail 50 --no-prompt

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~3s

stderr:

ERROR: VNext agents are currently enabled and require a session ID for log streaming.
Suggestion: Specify the session ID using --session, or run `azd ai agent invoke` first to create one

Also tried: azd ai agent monitor --type system --tail 50 --no-prompt — same error.

Classification: CLI bug / product limitation. azd ai agent monitor for VNext-enabled agents unconditionally requires a --session ID. There is no way to get container system logs or console output without first having a session from azd ai agent invoke. P05 (HOL-3715, Remote Invoke) is still in_progress, so no session ID exists yet for this template.

This is not an execution failure — the agent show command confirmed the agent is deployed and its metadata is correct.

steps.html:
P05 Remote Invoke completed with status FAIL. The azd ai agent invoke command returned HTTP 404 ResourceNotFound (Subdomain does not map to a resource) on the /endpoint/protocols/invocations path. The AI account resource exists and the base endpoint is reachable (HTTP 401), but the invocations protocol endpoint is not routing — likely a downstream effect of the P04 postdeploy RBAC hook failure (known azd bug). Not an identity/RBAC issue so retry rule did not apply. Full execution journal and structured artifact posted. No follow-up work identified.

Substep: monitor.azd_monitor_attempt — ❌ FAIL (CLI limitation)

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-15/echo-streaming-dotnet-invocations/src && azd ai agent monitor --no-prompt --tail 50

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~2s

Output (stderr):

ERROR: VNext agents are currently enabled and require a session ID for log streaming.
Suggestion: Specify the session ID using --session, or run `azd ai agent invoke` first to create one

Also attempted with --type system:

azd ai agent monitor --no-prompt --type system --tail 50

Same error — VNext agents require --session for all log types.

Classification: CLI bug / missing feature — azd ai agent monitor does not support session-less container log streaming for VNext-enabled agents. The --type system flag should arguably work without a session ID since system/container events are not session-scoped, but the CLI rejects it.

Workaround: None available without a session ID from a prior azd ai agent invoke. The agent show command confirms the agent is deployed and healthy.

Root cause is a template structural gap — the azure.yaml (ai-foundry-starter-basic) defines only infra/bicep, no services block of type azure.ai.agent. This is the same pattern across all templates using this base azure.yaml. Classified as template issue, not execution or auth failure. No follow-up work identified.

azd ai agent show echo-agent-responses-streaming --output json --no-prompt

Same error: no azure.ai.agent service named 'echo-agent-responses-streaming' found in azure.yaml

steps.objective: Run the sample locally and verify the protocol path.
steps.html:
P03 Local Run complete. azd ai agent run failed (Python 3.13 cgi module removal — template dependency bug). Workaround: manual venv with legacy-cgi + aiohttp>=3.10 succeeded. Both azd ai agent invoke --local --port 8091 and curl /responses returned correct echo response. All substeps verified. No follow-up work identified.

Substep: local_run.azd_run_attempt — ❌ FAIL

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-15/echo-streaming-py-responses/src && PORT=8091 azd ai agent run

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~8s (setup + immediate crash)

Output (key sections):

Using startup command: python main.py
Setting up Python environment...
Using CPython 3.13.5 interpreter
Creating virtual environment at: .venv
Installing dependencies (requirements.txt)...
  ✓ Dependencies installed (requirements.txt)

Starting agent on http://localhost:8088 (Ctrl+C to stop)

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'cgi'
...
ImportError: aiohttp package is not installed

ERROR: agent exited: exit status 1

Root cause: Python 3.13 removed the cgi module. The pinned aiohttp version in this template imports cgi, causing a cascading ImportError through azure-identity → azure-core → aiohttp → cgi.

Classification: Template/dependency bug — aiohttp version is incompatible with Python 3.13.

Also noted: PORT=8091 env var was ignored; azd defaults to port 8088.

Attempting workaround: Will try upgrading aiohttp in the venv or installing legacy-cgi package.

P05 Remote Invoke FAIL. Two distinct issues found: (1) azd CLI URL construction bug causing 404, (2) HTTP 424 upstream failure via correct path due to P04 postdeploy RBAC hook failure. RBAC retry rule applied (5-min wait + retry), same symptom — marked FAIL. Second invoke (state persistence test) skipped because first invoke never succeeded. No follow-up work identified — root causes are the known azd CLI bug and P04 RBAC hook failure.

Step 3: RBAC retry (5-minute wait) — FAIL

After waiting 5 minutes per the Identity/RBAC Retry Rule, retried the curl workaround:

Command:

curl -X POST 'https://ai-account-fpmm2jzurw5zy.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/ai-project-multiturn-dotnet-invocations/agents/multiturn-chat-invocations-dotnet/versions/9/invocations?api-version=2025-05-15-preview' -H 'Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"message": "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue."}'

Result: HTTP 424 (Failed Dependency) — same as before - x-adc-response-details: upstream - x-agent-invocation-id: inv_aa2742bbfb02022f00WCpv2jIQiVvH5kfhc7rBLZJuar3ldYE4 - x-agent-session-id: aa1a869ee6d3cd7600iqvcRdfzjIZOVGz9pAfFwyxxBLshmE5K - apim-request-id: 50bb07f5-23a2-4dd8-b7f9-64b55ec5b288 - Empty response body

Also retried azd ai agent invoke — still fails with 404 (azd CLI URL construction bug, separate issue).

Conclusion: FAIL per RBAC retry rule (same symptom after 5-min retry).

Root cause: P04 deploy's postdeploy RBAC hook failed (known AzureDeveloperCLICredential bug in JIT context). The agent container starts but cannot call Azure OpenAI because the managed identity lacks the required Cognitive Services role assignment. The 424 from Foundry confirms the upstream (agent container) is unreachable/failing.

Classification: Auth/RBAC issue — cascade from P04 postdeploy failure.

No second substep (remote_invoke.azd_invoke_attempt_2) was attempted because the first invoke never succeeded.

Step 2: First invoke — curl workaround (azd CLI bug workaround)

azd ai agent invoke constructs URL .../agents/{name}/endpoint/protocols/invocations which returns 404. The correct path is .../agents/{name}/versions/9/invocations?api-version=2025-05-15-preview (discovered via curl probing with correct https://ai.azure.com audience token).

Working URL (auth accepted):

POST https://ai-account-fpmm2jzurw5zy.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/ai-project-multiturn-dotnet-invocations/agents/multiturn-chat-invocations-dotnet/versions/9/invocations?api-version=2025-05-15-preview

Result: HTTP 424 (Failed Dependency) - x-adc-response-details: upstream — request reached agent container but it couldn't serve it - x-agent-session-id: 87515d24843d37b500xXOJ80mGdmETtGRZQIMA3utM13qqDgpu - x-agent-invocation-id: inv_e41ffeb1d897d44900oDLMGxl4QIhzSxfNl2zeCZUIGWcmIDYG - Empty response body - apim-request-id: 7ba88d84-6c00-43ef-b7e4-de4c9da889be

Root cause hypothesis: P04 deploy's postdeploy RBAC hook failed (known AzureDeveloperCLICredential bug), so the agent's managed identity likely lacks the Azure OpenAI Cognitive Services role assignment. The container starts but can't call the OpenAI backend.

Action: Applying RBAC retry rule — waiting 5 minutes before retry (in case roles were assigned by another mechanism and are still propagating).

Pre-flight passed.

steps.html:
P06 Monitor complete. azd ai agent show passed — agent is active with correct metadata (version 9, invocations protocol, correct image/env). azd ai agent monitor failed — requires session ID for VNext agents, and no session could be obtained due to (1) azd ai agent invoke URL bug (omits /versions/N → 404) and (2) direct REST invocations returning 424 Failed Dependency. These are known CLI/platform bugs affecting the entire invoke→monitor chain for VNext agents. No follow-up work identified — the invoke URL bug is already documented in P05.

Substep: monitor.azd_monitor_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-15/multiturn-dotnet-invocations/src && azd ai agent monitor --no-prompt

Exit code: 1 (reported as 0 by wrapper but output is an ERROR) Duration: <1s Status: ❌ FAIL — CLI bug

Output:

ERROR: VNext agents are currently enabled and require a session ID for log streaming.
Suggestion: Specify the session ID using --session, or run `azd ai agent invoke` first to create one

Analysis: azd ai agent monitor requires --session <id> for VNext hosted agents. This is a product/CLI limitation.

Attempted workarounds to obtain a session ID: 1. azd ai agent invoke hello --no-prompt → HTTP 404 (CLI URL construction bug — omits /versions/9 segment) 2. Direct REST POST to .../versions/9/invocations with ai.azure.com audience → HTTP 424 (Failed Dependency, empty body) 3. Direct REST POST with cognitiveservices.azure.com audience → HTTP 401 (wrong audience)

The 424 suggests the agent container is registered but not reachable/healthy for invocations. Without a successful invoke, no session ID can be obtained, making azd ai agent monitor impossible for this VNext agent.

All output written to /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-15/multiturn-dotnet-invocations/logs/P06-monitor.log.

steps.html:
P07 FAIL: Redeploy succeeded (v11) but invoke blocked by HTTP 424 upstream RBAC failure cascading from P04 postdeploy hook. azd CLI URL construction bug also confirmed. Log written to P07-update-redeploy.log.

P07 Update & Redeploy completed as FAIL.

Summary: - ✅ Code change: UPDATED appended to system prompt in Program.cs - ✅ Redeploy: azd deploy --no-prompt deployed v11 successfully - ❌ New session invoke: HTTP 424 (upstream RBAC failure — managed identity lacks Azure OpenAI access) - ⏭️ Reused session invoke: skipped (no valid session)

Two distinct issues found: 1. azd CLI URL construction bug — azd ai agent invoke builds .../endpoint/protocols/invocations which returns 404. Correct path is .../versions/{N}/invocations. 2. Postdeploy RBAC hook failure — AzureDeveloperCLICredential error prevents RBAC assignment. Agent container cannot call Azure OpenAI. Cascades from P04 through P05 and P07.

Additional harness issue: .azure/*/config.json was corrupted (0 bytes) between P04 and P07, requiring manual env var repopulation.

No follow-up work identified (the RBAC and CLI URL bugs are already known from P04/P05).

Both verified present in azd env get-values after setting.

P05 Remote Invoke completed with PARTIAL status.

Both canonical azd ai agent invoke commands executed successfully (exit 0). Session persistence confirmed — same session ID (6f41e4a1...) reused across both invocations. However, the agent response text is not printed by azd for /responses protocol agents, so we cannot verify whether the agent recalled "Alice" and "blue" from the first turn.

This is the known /responses protocol display bug in the azd ai agents extension (already tracked in known-bugs.yaml), not a template or product failure.

Evidence: P05-remote-invoke.log with full stdout/stderr captures, trace IDs, session verification.

No follow-up work identified — the /responses display bug is already tracked.

## P05 Execution Journal — Remote Invoke

### Pre-flight

Additional Verification Attempts

  1. --debug mode: Confirmed same behavior — debug output shows extension binary exit code 0 but no response text in any output stream
  2. Separate stdout/stderr capture: Confirmed response text appears in neither stdout nor stderr
  3. Hex dump of stdout: Confirmed no hidden/non-printable response text
  4. Direct curl to agent endpoint: Could not authenticate — the /responses protocol endpoint requires a specific auth flow that azd handles internally but is not documented for direct API calls
  5. .foundry-agent.json verified: Session state correctly persisted between invokes, confirming the same session ID

Assessment

Both invocations completed successfully (exit 0). The session was correctly assigned on invoke 1 and reused on invoke 2, confirming multi-turn session persistence at the protocol level. However, we cannot verify whether the agent actually recalled Alice/blue because azd ai agent invoke does not print the response text for /responses protocol agents. Substep: update_redeploy.azd_deploy_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-15/multiturn-py-responses/src && azd deploy --no-prompt

Issue: The azd environment state from P02 provision was not persisted to the local .azure/<env>/.env file. The provision was done in a tmux session whose state was lost after P04 completed.

Recovery attempts: 1. Set AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID, AZURE_LOCATION, AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ID, AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT, AZURE_CONTAINER_REGISTRY_ENDPOINT manually via azd env set 2. Created new multiturn-py-responses env and set all vars 3. Deploy completed with SUCCESS but reported "less than a second" with no actual service packaging/publishing steps — azd did not discover the agent service to redeploy

Root cause: The azure.yaml has no explicit services: section — azd AI agent extension dynamically discovers services via the agent.yaml file during provision, and this state is only available in the session that ran provision. Without the full provision output state (including service mappings), azd deploy thinks there are no services.

Exit code: 0 (misleading — no actual deploy occurred) Duration: ~1s Status: ❌ FAIL — Redeploy could not execute because azd provision state was not persisted across phases. This is a harness/env-state issue, not a product failure.
